questions.question: Explain the working of a 4-to-1 multiplexer with the help of its truth table and logic circuit diagram. How does it select one input from multiple inputs based on select lines?
keyPoints: 4-to-1 MUX has 4 data inputs (I0, I1, I2, I3) and 2 select lines (S0, S1)
Output Y = I0.S1'.S0' + I1.S1'.S0 + I2.S1.S0' + I3.S1.S0
Select lines determine which input is routed to output based on binary combination
Can be implemented using AND gates, OR gates, and NOT gates (or using transmission gates)

questions.question: Describe the difference between a decoder and an encoder. Explain the working of a 3-to-8 decoder with its truth table and mention one practical application of decoders in digital systems.
keyPoints: Decoder converts n-bit binary input into 2^n unique outputs (demultiplexing)
Encoder converts 2^n inputs into n-bit binary output (compresses data)
3-to-8 decoder activates one of 8 outputs based on 3-bit input code
Practical applications: memory addressing, chip selection, seven-segment display driving

questions.question: (a) Design a full adder circuit using half adders and OR gates. Explain how it performs addition of three bits (A, B, and Cin) producing Sum and Carry_out. (b) Compare Ripple Carry Adder and Carry Look-Ahead Adder in terms of speed, hardware complexity, and propagation delay. Why is Carry Look-Ahead Adder faster than Ripple Carry Adder?
keyPoints: Full adder: Sum = A XOR B XOR Cin, Carry_out = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B))
Can be constructed using two half adders and one OR gate (first half adder adds A,B, second adds result to Cin)
Ripple Carry Adder: cascaded full adders, delay increases linearly with number of bits (propagation through each stage)
Carry Look-Ahead Adder: computes carry bits in parallel using generate and propagate functions, reducing delay to O(log n)

questions.question: (a) Design a circuit to implement a 2-to-4 decoder using basic logic gates. Explain how enable inputs (active HIGH and active LOW) affect the decoder operation. (b) Implement a 4-to-1 multiplexer using only 2-to-1 multiplexers. Show the connections and explain the selection logic. (c) A priority encoder has four inputs D3, D2, D1, D0 (D3 has highest priority). Derive the truth table and write expressions for outputs A1 and A0.
keyPoints: 2-to-4 decoder: uses NOT and AND gates, each output corresponds to one input combination, enable=1 activates all outputs
Active LOW enable: outputs are all HIGH when enable=0, selected output goes LOW; active HIGH: selected output goes HIGH when enable=1
4-to-1 MUX using 2-to-1 MUX: first level - two 2-to-1 MUXs for inputs I0,I1 and I2,I3 with S0 as select; second level - output of first level MUXs selected by S1
Priority encoder truth table: D3=1 gives A1A0=11, D3=0,D2=1 gives 10, D3=D2=0,D1=1 gives 01, all zero gives 00
